summary_one_line: Input must parse as valid SQL containing a '||' string-concatenation expression so the VDBE compiles and executes OP_Concat.

hypotheses.what_input_feature: A well-formed SQL statement whose expression tree contains a string concatenation, i.e. the literal '||' operator (bytes 7c 7c) between two text operands inside a SELECT, so the parser emits an OP_Concat opcode that the VDBE reaches at /src/sqlite3/bld/sqlite3.c:86705.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: All three winner-resolving seeds are syntactically valid SELECT statements containing '||': seed 1 has 'SELECT 11-0133=1...3=1||3=13' with 7c 7c at offset 0x32, and seeds 2 and 3 carry 'SELECT'''L''''''' followed by '||' (7c 7c) at offsets 0x11-0x12; the BYTE DIFF confirms W concentrates on 7c(|)x2 at offsets 0x11 and 0x12 and on the SELECT keyword bytes 53(S)/45(E)/4c(L)/45(E)/43(C)/54(T) at offsets 0x00-0x05.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: The naive loser-blocking seeds are non-parsing byte garbage (e.g. 'T 1; c BINARY' or '``g..;......y' or high-byte blobs) with no SELECT keyword and no '||' operator at any offset, so they never compile to a program containing OP_Concat and OP_Concat stays at T=0 in the loser column.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: value_profile adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket) over intercepted CMPs in the SQL tokenizer/parser; inputs that match more of the 'SELECT' keyword prefix or get a closer byte match on the '||' token produce a new closer CMP_MAP bucket and are kept as corpus, letting value_profile climb the gradient toward syntactically valid concatenation SQL that naive's plain edge feedback cannot assemble by luck.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocked true side is the OP_Concat VDBE case, the opcode for SQL string concatenation '||'.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: case OP_Concat: {           /* same as TK_CONCAT, in1, in2, out3 */

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile is the decisive winner over naive at this branch.
value_profile resolves the branch in 9/10 trials while naive stays blocked in all 10.
Winner seed 2/3 contains a SELECT statement with the '||' concatenation operator.
Winner seed 1 contains a valid SELECT with '||' at offset 0x32.
Loser naive seeds are non-SQL byte garbage with no SELECT keyword or '||' operator.
At the SQL-keyword offsets the winner concentrates on the SELECT bytes while the loser is dispersed across non-keyword bytes.
At offsets 0x11-0x12 the winner concentrates on the '||' operator bytes that the loser never produces.
value_profile rewards inputs whose intercepted-CMP operands are closer to matching by adding a new CMP_MAP bucket and preserving them.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
An input that produces a
CMP-operand pair closer to matching than any previously-seen pair
adds a new CMP_MAP bucket and is preserved as corpus.

evidence_trail.claim: The value_profile CMP_MAP bucket is keyed on operand-distance, a coarse encoding of byte/prefix closeness.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
each CMP callback writes into a `CMP_MAP` keyed by (PC, operand-distance
bucket)

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is CMP_MAP gradient (decisive delta is value_profile, not I2S), so db_query.py lineage is not required; the prompt's MECHANISM CONTEXT states value_profile uses no I2SRandReplace and emits no dash rows, so an I2S-floor lineage check is inapplicable here.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where value_profile resolves OP_Concat no faster than naive even though the only path to OP_Concat requires emitting a valid SELECT...||... query, indicating CMP_MAP keyword/operator gradient is not what drives the divergence.

weakest_evidence_point: The CMP_MAP gradient story assumes the SQL tokenizer's keyword/operator matching goes through intercepted integer/string CMPs that feed value_profile's distance buckets, which is inferred from mechanism text rather than observed in the parser's CMP instrumentation.
